title: Reproducibility Handout for the Life Sciences
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6oalb","text":"This handout is based on the Caltech Reproducibility resource that was created by Code Ocean, Addgene, and protocols.io. It is extended to cover more tools and resources for biomedical scientists specifically.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4b0jn","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b4che","text":"Please feel free to clone and modify it. If you do, would be wonderful to see you share the new resource in this group. Also, please suggest other useful resources.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

Plan for reproducibility before you start","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":44}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4kc5c","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fcb8g","text":"Write a study plan or protocol and track new versions.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":30}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7rdc5","text":"Set-up a reproducible project using an electronic lab notebook to organize and track your work. Avoid saving proprietary file formats.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":30}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1m8m6","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"91lkn","text":"2. Free tools to help you make your first registration include AsPredicted, Open Science Framework, and Registered Reports. steps: Addgene addgene.org (nonprofit plasmid repository) ICLAC iclac.org (registry of false or misidentified cell lines) Quartzy quartzy.com (manage lab inventory) RRID scicrunch.org/resources (persistent and unique identifiers for referencing a research resource)

Benchling benchling.com (free) Evernote evernote.com (most popular with biologists but not designed as an ELN) Labguru labguru.com ($) sciNote scinote.net/ (open source, free) Open Science Framework osf.io/ (free)
Bio-Protocol bio-protocol.org (A peer-reviewed protocol journal; free to read & publish) protocols.io protocols.io (an open access repository of science methods; free to read & publish)

Github github.com (code repository; free for public repos) Jupyter Notebooks jupyter.org (open source web-app for creating & sharing live code, equations, and more) Code Ocean codeocean.com/ (computational reproducibility platform; free to upload, share & publish executable code with DOI; pay for more computing time over freemium limit)

DataDryad datadryad.org (curated digital repository; free to access, $120 to publish dataset up to 20GB) Figshare figshare.com (free digital repository, 5GB per file limit) Zenodo zenodo.org (free digital repository; 50GB per dataset limit)

BenchSci benchsci.com (antibody search engine with published figures) Bioz bioz.com (search engine for life science reagents, tools, kits, instruments) CiteAb citeab.com (antibody search engine with results sorted by citations) antYbuddY antybuddy.com (antibody & protein review platform)

Elemental Machines elementalmachines.io (sensors and monitoring of lab equipment) Opentrons opentrons.com (liquid handling robot, for $3K-$4K) ScienceExchange scienceexchange.com (marketplace of research outsourcing providers) TetraScience tetrascience.com (sensors and monitoring of lab equipment) NC3Rs: nc3rs.org.uk/experimental-design-assistant-eda (free online experimental design assistant)
creator: Lenny Teytelman
